characters.name: Monsieur Alfonse
characters.description: Monsieur AlfonseJob/positionUndertaker & Deputy MayorNationalityFrenchProduction informationFirst appearanceThe FuneralLast appearanceA Winkle In TimePortrayed byKenneth Connor  MonsieurAlfonseis afictional characterin theBBCsitcom'Allo 'Allo!, which ran from 1982 to 1992. He was played byactorKenneth Connor. Monsieur Alfonse is half-french/half-belgian[1]local ofNouvion' and the townsundertaker, first being seen when local heroRené Artoisfakes his own death. Alfonse quickly falls in love with the latter's wife,Edith, and René has to propose to Edith or lose his café as everyone believes he is dead. Alfonse also has a small fortune in aSwiss bankaccount; therefore,Edith's motheragrees with the match, as Alfonse would make a better husband financially. The undertaker's catchphrase is"Swiftly and with style", while announcing his services. In addition, he also has a "dicky ticker", which comes on every time something stressful (or arousing) happens, and also suffers from the less publicised "tricky truss". He admires René for all the brave work he does forthe Resistance, and agrees to admire Edith from afar. When he finds out about René's affair with waitressYvettehe tells Edith, but René manages to fob her off with another excuse.

characters.name: Small characters in 'Allo 'Allo!
characters.description: The series 'Allo 'Allo! contains several smaller characters who are only mentioned by name or just briefly seen in a episode. These characters have little or no effect on the overall story through the series. A list of all characters appeared in 'Allo 'Allo! can beseen here. Listed in alphabetic order. Unless other is stated, characters in the list is only mentioned by name. Called up to byRené Artois.[1]

characters.name: Rene Artois Jr.
characters.description: Rene Artois Jr.Series9Job/positionCafe OwnerNationalityFrenchProduction informationFirst appearanceA Winkle In TimeLast appearanceA Winkle In TimePortrayed byGorden Kaye  Rene Artois Jr.is the "adopted" son ofRené ArtoisandYvette Carte-Blanchebut it is implied that he is Rene's natural child. After the second world war he took over the businessCafé René.

characters.name: Rene Artois Jr.
characters.description: Rene Artois Jr.Series9Job/positionCafe OwnerNationalityFrenchProduction informationFirst appearanceA Winkle In TimeLast appearanceA Winkle In TimePortrayed byGorden Kaye  Rene Artois Jr.is the "adopted" son ofRené ArtoisandYvette Carte-Blanchebut it is implied that he is Rene's natural child. After the second world war he took over the businessCafé René.

characters.name: Edith Artois
characters.description: Edith ArtoisSeriesAllo AlloAliasesMrs.NighthawkJob/positionPart owner of Cafe Rene(later claimed as full owner after the apparent death of Rene)NationalityFrenchAffiliationThe French ResistanceAgeUncertain (claims to be 27)Production informationFirst appearancePilot episode- The British are comingLast appearanceA Winkle in TimePortrayed byCarmen Silvera  The main antagonist of 'Allo 'Allo, Edith Melba Artois is the wife ofRene Artois, daughter ofFanny La Fanand the cabaret performer at the 'Café René'. Edith is the wife (and subsequently, sister-in-law) of her husbandRené Artois. After Rene'sexecutionand fake death, she became the legal owner of thecafe, which gave her greater influence over her husband's actions than she had before. She does however dream of the day when René will remarry her (as his own falsetwinbrother). Madame Edith, despite beingtone deaf, fancies herself acabaretsinger, and insists on treating cafe customers to displays of her talents. Her concerts usually clear the cafe; those who remain stuffcheesein their ears to muffle her wails. While one of the waitresses,Mimi Labonq, actually does have talent, Madame Artois will not usually permit her to entertain the customers. Madame Edith’s singing is so horrible that she has shattered glass. In one duet, she made every dog in the town howl, and when several Resistance girls where going to shoot Renè as a collaborator. He made her sing a few lines of song as proof that he was no collaborator.  She has no problem with the waitresses taking favoured customers upstairs, though. . Madame Edith is a jealous wife, and will become angry and suspicious of Rene whenever he is near other women, especially Yvette, Rene's true love However she is very easy to hoodwink, whenever she catches her husband in a clinch with one of the waitresses, she swallows whatever transparent lies Rene chooses to tell her. Since she is thought to be a widow, she occasionally has suitors of her own, who flatter her enormously. They includeMonsieur Alfonse, the local undertaker; andBertorelli, an Italian captain. Edith's relationship with Rene himself can be quite contradictory at times, at one point she was quite ready to commit a suicide bombing to save Rene from the Germans and on other occasions is quite eager to see Rene die in some heroic manner so she can be considered the widow of a great man, showing just how selfish she is. Edith appeared in the pilot episode and every subsequent episode. She is sometimes the butt of several jokes about herlooksand exaggerating herage. Her age was at one point suggested to be over a hundred years old as she claimed she entertained soldiers returning from War and Rene riposted that it was during theCrimean War. In general, Edith is an opportunistic, pompous, extremely self-important, selfish, extravagant, incredibly arrogant, and callous sycophant, as shown when she worries about having Rene's twin brother's will leaving everything to her as opposed to Monsieur Alfonse's

characters.name: René Artois
characters.description: René ArtoisSeries1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9AliasesNighthawk, René 1, René 2, The Grand Master Big HootJob/positionCafe OwnerNationalityFrenchAffiliationThe French ResistanceProduction informationFirst appearanceThe British Are ComingLast appearanceThe Return of 'Allo 'Allo!Portrayed byGorden Kaye René François Artoisis the owner ofCafé Renéin theFrenchtown ofNouvion. He is the husband ofEdith Artoisand is a womanizer to his WaitressesYvette Carte-BlancheandMaria Recamierand Mimi Labonq. Whilst having a seemingly quiet life and befriending Germans. Rene is offered the chance to be a pivotal player in theFrench Resistanceby current leaderMichelle Dubois. In addition, he is also charged with the protection ofthe Fallen Madonna paintingbyColonel Kurt Von Strohm. Desperately trying to hide away all these incriminating factors, Rene tries to survive the war without any danger and eventually marry his waitress/mistress Yvette, but is determined to marry Edith first in order to prolong his rightful hold on the cafe. René was born in Nancy, where also had a fling with Denise Laroque, which saves his life when she recognises him by a mole when the communist resistance wants to shoot him as a collaborator. He has a cousin in Bordeaux[1] Rene is stubborn, sarcastic and reluctant, but can sometimes see himself good at something. He is also a coward and the only way the Resistance or the Germans are using him is by threatening to shoot him. He is, however, very caring.

characters.name: Von Beckstein
characters.description: Von Becksteinis a fictional German army soldier, asmall characterin the series'Allo 'Allo!. At some point the name was assumed byRoger LeClercwhen he was disguised as a soldier wearing a German uniform. It is suggested that Von Beckstein is either, a name made up or a "real" German soldier, but never seen on screen. The latter is supported in a interrogation byDenise Laroqueof two assumed German soldiers with stolen uniforms (Rene ArtoisandRoger LeClerc) where LeClerc claims to be Von Beckstein[1]. It is assumed that the uniform carried some sort of identification of Von Beckstein.

Unless other is stated, characters in the list is only mentioned by name. Called up to byRené Artois.[1]

characters.name: Alberto Bertorelli
characters.description: Captain Alberto BertorelliJob/positionCaptainNationalityItalianProduction informationFirst appearanceIt's Raining ItaliansLast appearanceRene Of The GypsiesPortrayed byGavin Richards (Series 4-6)Roger Kitter (Series 7) Roger Kitter (Series 7)  Bertorelli was, as a result ofNazi Germany's alliance withItalyandBenito Mussolini, madeColonel Kurt Von Strohm's new assistant, afterHans Geeringwas taken toBritainin season 4. Before the war, he was a gondolier in Venice. Having something of an eye for the ladies, Bertorelli's main catchphrase was "What a mistake-a to make-a!", after the occasional blunder. His other main saying consisted in a happy "Heil-a Mussolini!", when theGerman commandsaid "Heil Hitler!". It was usually accepted with a sarcastic dismissal or, sometimes, even anger. He also thought that he was very popular and always asked ladies if they would "like a good time with the-a Italian hero". He also pretended to love Edith so he could marry her and get his hands on the cafe. He is pompous, highly self-obsessed, very annoying and extremely vain, with hopelessly cowardly characteristics. He always says 'Alberto' when referring to himself, and has the habit of pointlessly kissing people he meets during greeting. He even pulls off an incident when he first meets Rene: Rene: I am just a simple cafe owner trying to lead a life with my wife and mother in law. Alberto approaches and embraces Yvette: I embrace-a your wife. Alberto approaches and nearly embraces Edith: I embrace-a your mother in law... Edith(With an arrogant and furious expression): I am the wife! Alberto: What-a mistake-a to make-a! The Italian captain is not a beloved character in the eyes of the German officers. e.g. the Colonel, the Lieutenant and Private Helga try and keep the painting of 'The fallen madonna' secret for as long as possible from him and whenever he greets the General with a 'Heil-a-Mussolini' the General usually answers with: 'not on your nelly!'.

characters.name: Michelle Dubois
characters.description: Michelle DuboisSeries1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9AliasesBlue Tit, Michelle of the ResistanceJob/positionLeader of the de Gaulle Resistance/ Post MistressNationalityFrenchAffiliationThe de Gaulle ResistanceProduction informationPortrayed byKirsten Cooke  Michelle Dubois(also known as Michelle of theResistance) is a main character in 'Allo 'Allo, played byKirsten Cooke. She is the leader of the predominantly female de Gaulle Resistance.[1]She is one of the few characters in the series that is bi-lingual. She pretended to be attracted to René to keep him in the Resistance. Her code name wasBlue Tit. A true believer in the cause of the Resistance, the light of fanaticism drives her dedication to her guerrilla activities, but also makes her expect the same of the others. Those activities primarily consist of concocting wild plan after wild plan (usually to get the British airmen back to England, but with the occasional harassment of the occupying Germans thrown in), that then usually fall upon René and the others to try and execute. Michelle is one of the few regular female characters who appears resistant to René's charms, though she is willing to pretend an interest on occasion, to get him to do what she wants.

characters.name: "Bumbly" Carstairs
characters.description: RAF Flying Officer "Bumbly" CarstairsJob/positionFlying OfficerNationalityBritishAffiliationRoyal Army ForceProduction informationFirst appearancePilot: The British Are ComingLast appearanceA Winkle In TimePortrayed byNicholas Frankau  RAF Flying OfficerBumbly Carstairsis a fictional character in the BBC sitcom'Allo 'Allo!, which ran from 1982 to 1992. He was portrayed by the actor Nicholas Frankau.

characters.name: Yvette Carte-Blanche
characters.description: Yvette Carte-BlancheAliasesWomen of the streetsJob/positionWaitress/Prostitute/Mistress of ReneNationalityFrenchAffiliationCollaborator (French Resistance)Age19Production informationFirst appearancePilot: The British Are ComingLast appearanceA Winkle In TimePortrayed byVicki Michelle  Yvette Carte-Blancheis a main character in 'Allo 'Allo, and works as the head waitress inCafé René, originally alongsideMaria Recamierand laterMimi Labonq. She is passionately in love withRenéand is found kissing and hugging him nearly every episode. She would do anything for the only man she could ever love.

These characters have little or no effect on the overall story through the series. A list of all characters appeared in 'Allo 'Allo! can beseen here. Listed in alphabetic order. Unless other is stated, characters in the list is only mentioned by name. Called up to byRené Artois.[1]

characters.name: Officer Crabtree
characters.description:
Officer CrabtreeSeriesS2E03 - S9E06Job/positionSecret Agent & French PolicemanNationalityBritishAffiliationde Gaulle ResistanceProduction informationFirst appearanceThe Poloceman Cometh(S2E03)Last appearanceThe Return of 'Allo 'Allo!Portrayed byArthur Bostrum  OfficerCrabtreeis afictional characterin theBBCsitcom'Allo 'Allo!, which ran from 1982 to 1992; he was played by actorArthur Bostrom. The character is an undercover British agent masquerading as agendarme. His grasp ofFrenchis limited however, and this is represented on screen by his mangling ofvowelsfor comic effect. Crabtree is occasionally heard speaking his maternal English, mostly when speaking toFairfaxandCarstairs. His bad French also seems to be contagious as several French characters (particularlyMadame Edith) end up speaking like him without realising it after hearing him talk. It is also of note that others, especiallyRené, will use his mangled French to insult him, such as in "Pass off" (for "Piss off"). A running gag throughout the series is that, when any of the other characters meet British officers (even Fairfax and Carstairs on one occasion, when they were to be disguised as gendarmes) their French is just the same as Crabtree's. After the character's introduction, various episodes were named in this talking manners (e.g. "Up The Crick Without A Piddle" for "Up the Creek without a Paddle" in Season 7).

characters.name: Engelbert Von Smallhausen
characters.description: Herr Engelbert Von SmallhausenAliasesVon CrackenfartJob/positionGestapo OfficerNationalityGermanProduction informationFirst appearanceThe DuelLast appearanceA Winkle In TimePortrayed byJohn Louis Mansi HerrEngelbert Von Smallhausenis afictional characterin the series'Allo 'Allo!.In the beginning, he was only credited as "Gestapo man". His real name is revealed to be Bobby Cedric[1]. he is Herr flick  identical twin brother.to Herr flick Von Smallhausen is not the brightest of individuals, and is persistently mistreated by his superior,Herr Otto Flick- who sometimes hits Von Smallhausen over the head with a rubber truncheon, and it is implied in some episodes that he resides in a small cupboard at Flick's headquarters. On a later occasion he reveals that he has a'small but comfortable'apartment over the bakery)[2].

characters.name: Small characters in 'Allo 'Allo!
characters.description: The series 'Allo 'Allo! contains several smaller characters who are only mentioned by name or just briefly seen in a episode. These characters have little or no effect on the overall story through the series. A list of all characters appeared in 'Allo 'Allo! can beseen here. Listed in alphabetic order. Unless other is stated, characters in the list is only mentioned by name. Called up to byRené Artois.[1]

characters.name: Michelle Dubois
characters.description: Michelle DuboisSeries1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9AliasesBlue Tit, Michelle of the ResistanceJob/positionLeader of the de Gaulle Resistance/ Post MistressNationalityFrenchAffiliationThe de Gaulle ResistanceProduction informationPortrayed byKirsten Cooke  Michelle Dubois(also known as Michelle of theResistance) is a main character in 'Allo 'Allo, played byKirsten Cooke. She is the leader of the predominantly female de Gaulle Resistance.[1]She is one of the few characters in the series that is bi-lingual. She pretended to be attracted to René to keep him in the Resistance. Her code name wasBlue Tit. A true believer in the cause of the Resistance, the light of fanaticism drives her dedication to her guerrilla activities, but also makes her expect the same of the others. Those activities primarily consist of concocting wild plan after wild plan (usually to get the British airmen back to England, but with the occasional harassment of the occupying Germans thrown in), that then usually fall upon René and the others to try and execute. Michelle is one of the few regular female characters who appears resistant to René's charms, though she is willing to pretend an interest on occasion, to get him to do what she wants.

characters.name: Gavin Fairfax
characters.description: RAF Flight Lieutenant Gavin FairfaxJob/positionRAF OfficerNationalityBritishAffiliationRoyal Air ForceProduction informationFirst appearancePilot: The British Are ComingLast appearanceA Winkle In TimePortrayed byJohn D. Collins RAF Flight LieutenantGavin Fairfaxare afictional characterin theBBCsitcom'Allo 'Allo!, which ran from 1982 to 1992. He is portrayed by the actorJohn D. Collins. In the series pilot episode it was told that he and his comradeCarstairshad been shot down. They were later found by the Resistance, who forcedRené Artois(the owner of Café René) to keep them in hiding after the owner of their previous safe house had been shot. There were many failed attempts by the Resistance to get the pilots back to England and they had to remain in hiding throughout the entire show.

characters.name: Fanny La Fan
characters.description: Fanny La FanFanny La Fan as played by Rose HillAge84Significant othersRoger LeClerc(former lover),Ernest LeClerc(Husband), Countless othersFamily membersEdith Artois(daughter),Rene Artois(son-in-law)Production informationPortrayed byRose Hill  Madam Fanny La Fanis the mother ofEdith Artoisand therefore mother-in-law toRene Artois. She was played by the actressRose Hill. An elderly woman, Fanny is also a misanthrope who often moans at anything or anyone with whom she comes into contact. Even her daughterEdith, who is one of the few people who makes an effort to be nice to her, can rarely meet her constant demands or appease her crankiness.She only can hear with her ear trumpet. She often uses a walking stick to bang on the floor of her bedroom so that the other characters below will hear and come to her assistance. More often than not, however, the reasons for the calls for help are nothing more than complaints about what was served for lunch. Fanny and her son-in-lawRenéshare a mutual hatred of each other (the only time she is pleasant to Rene is when she learns of a large quantity of gold in his possession and then encourages Edith to marry him). Despite this, however, they are both constantly being caught up together in the plans of the French, English or German intelligence - René because of how "out of the way" his cafe is, and Fanny because since she is by and large bed-bound. While it is sometimes unclear whose side René is really on, Fanny is fiercely patriotic and extremely anti-German always spitting three times on the sight or mention of them. The only time she did not is whenGeneral Von Klinkerhoffenoffered her some wine.[1] Unhappily, she carries the same characteristics as her daughter. She is arrogant, selfish, unintelligent and callous. She is terrified of Monsieur Alfonse, mainly due to the fact that he is an undertaker and she does not want to die yet. She went to the extent of throwing three flower pots at him from her window.

characters.name: Leopold Von Flockenstuffen
characters.description: General Leopold Von FlockenstuffenJob/positionGeneralNationalityGermanProduction informationFirst appearanceWatch The BirdieLast appearanceAn Almighty BangPortrayed byKen Morley  General Leopold Von Flockenstuffenis afictional characterin theBBCsitcom'Allo 'Allo!, which ran from 1982 to 1992. He was played by the actorKen Morley. He appeared in six episodes of thefifth,sixthandseventhseasons of the series.

characters.name: Hubert Gruber
characters.description: Hubert GruberJob/positionLieutenant (later Oberleutnant)NationalityGermanAffiliationGerman armyAgeIn his 20s-30s, not specified.Production informationFirst appearance"Pilot episode- The British are coming"Portrayed byGuy Siner  Lieutnant (later Oberleutnant)Hubert Gruberis one of the main German characters in'Allo 'Allo!. From the very beginning of the series he shows a strong affection to René Artois, which becomes a running gag during the whole series. He is assigned as an ADC (aide-de-camp) toGeneral Von Klinkerhoffenand later as an unoffical assistant toColonel Von StrohmwhenCaptain Hans Geeringgoing missing. He lives at the Chateau[1].

characters.name: Heinrich Himmler
characters.description: Heinrich HimmlerwasReich Leader(Reichsführer) of theProtection Squadron(Schutzstaffel) and a leading member of the Nazi Party of Nazi Germany. During the series'Allo 'Allo!Himmler is mentioned several times byHerr Otto Flickof the Gestapo, and from time to also receiving letters from Himmler about his progress of finding the painting ofThe Fallen Madonnaor other failed attempts. Herr Flick often proudly refer to him as his godfather as a way to increase his own authority. It is also revealed that Himmler is the reason why Herr Flick joined the Gestapo.

characters.name: Adolf Hitler
characters.description: Adolf Hitlerwas an Austrian-born German politician and the leader of the Nazi Party. He was chancellor of Germany from 1933 to 1945 and dictator of Nazi Germany (as Führer und Reichskanzler) from 1934 to 1945. According toHerr Otto Flickof the Gestapo, Adolf Hitler wanted the paintingThe Fallen MadonnabyVan Klompto give to Eva Braun[1]. Herr Flick uses this as his motivation to find the painting and send it to Hitler. Herr Flick misunderstands a conversation betweenVon StrohmandVon Klinkerhoffen, believing they plan to assassinate Hitler[2].

characters.name: Otto Flick
characters.description: Herr Otto FlickAliasesIrma Von Kinkenrotten, Captain HeisengerkinJob/positionGestapo OfficerNationalityGermanAffiliationGestapoProduction informationFirst appearanceThe British Are ComingLast appearanceA Winkle In TimePortrayed byRichard Gibson(Series 1-8)David Janson(Series 9) David Janson(Series 9)

characters.name: Erich Von Klinkerhoffen
characters.description:
Erich Von KlinkerhoffenSeriesAllJob/positionGeneralNationalityGermanAffiliationWehrmachtAgeIn his 40s-50s, not specified.Production informationFirst appearanceThe ExecutionLast appearanceA Winkle In TimePortrayed byHilary Minister Erich Von Klinkerhoffenis a General in theWehrmachtand isGruber'sandVon Strohm'ssuperior. Von Klinkerhoffen has small eyes and small lips and balding grey hair.
He wears red staff tabs and hasepaulettesof aGeneralleutnant. He is decorated with theIron Crossand bar, and considered a hero from the Russian front[1]. The General is a very direct person and often threatens his men with a ticket to theRussian Front. He doesn't seem to care about any of his men, once refusing to pay the ransom to release Von Strohm and Gruber from theCommunist Resistance. He has even less of a liking for the Gestapo, and forcedHerr Flickat gunpoint to release Von Strohm and his aide when under Gestapo detention. Not a lot is known about the general, although he does like women quite a lot, he tried to seduceMariawhen he found her in his chateau. In some episodes, he has caught the other officers "having a good time" with one of the waitresses. However, at one point he invited Lt. Gruber to a party at the Chateau, commenting there was no need to change his clothes - Gruber was dressed as a lady at the time. Despite this, von Klinkerhoffen is a very egotistical and narcissistic man, and his arrogance often gets the better of him.
